reasoning: The TGA balance is typically driven by regular cycles of tax receipts and outlays. Mid-October includes a tax deadline for extension filers, generally pushing the TGA balance up (as seen in the high $794B level on Oct 16). After this, the TGA generally draws down until the next major tax receipt date in mid-December. Specifically, between October 26 and November 2, heavy beginning-of-month outlays (Social Security, military pay, etc.) typically hit on the 1st of the month, which will fall on a Friday (Oct 31) this year. This makes a decrease by Nov 2 highly likely. Throughout November, the balance tends to stay lower because it's a deficit month with no major tax revenues. By January 24, the mid-December and mid-January tax receipts will have occurred, increasing the chance of the balance rebounding, bringing the probability closer to 50%. Our probabilities naturally reflect increasing uncertainty over time, pulling closer to the 0.50 anchor.
